title: Grammangis huttonii
intro: Grammangis huttonii, also known as the Indian swamp cypress, is a plant species native to tropical forests in India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Grammangis huttonii include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Grammangis huttonii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat conditions such as bronchitis, coughs, and skin infections.
taxonomy: Grammangis huttonii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Grammangis, which is a small genus of orchids. The classification of Grammangis huttonii places it within the subfamily Epidendroideae. Within this subfamily, Grammangis huttonii is further classified as part of the tribe Cymbidieae. This classification is based on morphological and molecular studies of the plant.

In traditional medicine, Grammangis huttonii is used to treat fever, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Grammangis huttonii for medicinal purposes.
